title: Implement full Home.tsx page with post feed, comments display, and add comment functionality

requirements.text: Update Home.tsx to fetch posts from backend using backendActor.getFeed() and display them in a feed layout with max-w-2xl centered container on gray-100 background

quotes: Fetches posts from your backend canister

acceptanceCriteria: Home page calls backendActor.getFeed() to retrieve posts array
Posts are displayed in a centered container with max-w-2xl width
Page uses min-h-screen bg-gray-100 for full-height gray background
Page title 'RunInsta Feed' is displayed with text-2xl font-bold mb-4 styling

requirements.text: Implement automatic feed refresh in Home.tsx that polls backendActor.getFeed() every 5 seconds using setInterval in useEffect
acceptanceCriteria: useEffect sets up interval that calls fetchPosts every 5000ms
Interval is cleared on component unmount using cleanup function
Feed updates automatically to show new posts and comments without manual refresh

requirements.text: Display comments section below each PostCard in Home.tsx showing all existing comments with author name and text, using font-semibold for author and text-sm text-gray-700 for comment text
acceptanceCriteria: Comments section has 'Comments' heading with font-semibold text-sm text-gray-600 styling
Each comment displays author name in font-semibold followed by colon
Comment text uses text-sm text-gray-700 styling
All comments from post.comments array are rendered

requirements.text: Add comment input form below comments list in Home.tsx with text input field and 'Post' button, supporting Enter key submission and per-post comment text state management
acceptanceCriteria: Input field has 'Add a comment...' placeholder text
Input uses flex-1 border rounded-l-md px-2 py-1 focus:outline-none styling
Submit button labeled 'Post' with bg-blue-500 text-white px-3 rounded-r-md hover:bg-blue-600
Pressing Enter key in input triggers handleAddComment
Clicking 'Post' button triggers handleAddComment
Each post maintains separate comment text state using newComments object keyed by post.id

requirements.text: Implement handleAddComment function in Home.tsx that calls backendActor.addComment(postId, text), clears the input field, and refreshes the feed

quotes: fetchPosts(); // refresh feed to show new comment

acceptanceCriteria: handleAddComment validates that comment text is not empty before proceeding
Function calls backendActor.addComment with postId and text parameters
After successful comment addition, input field for that post is cleared
fetchPosts is called after addComment to refresh feed and display new comment

requirements.text: Implement addComment backend function in main.mo that accepts postId (Nat), text (Text), and author (Principal), creates comment with timestamp, adds it to the post's comments list, and returns Bool indicating success

quotes: public func addComment(postId: Nat, text: Text, author: Principal) : async Bool
let comment = { author; text; createdAt = Time.now() };

acceptanceCriteria: addComment function signature accepts postId (Nat), text (Text), and author (Principal)
Function iterates through posts array to find matching postId
When post found, creates comment record with author, text, and createdAt (Time.now())
Comment is appended to post's comments array using Array.append
Returns true if post found and comment added, false otherwise

requirements.text: Define TypeScript type interfaces in Home.tsx for Comment with author (string), text (string), and createdAt (number), and Post with id (number), author (any/Principal), content (string), mediaUrl (string), likes (any[]), comments (Comment[]), and createdAt (number)

quotes: type Comment = { author: string; text: string; createdAt: number; };
type Post = { id: number; author: any; content: string; mediaUrl: string; likes: any[]; comments: Comment[]; createdAt: number; };

acceptanceCriteria: Comment type defined with author as string, text as string, createdAt as number
Post type defined with id as number, author as any (Principal), content, mediaUrl, likes array, comments array, and createdAt
Type definitions are placed at top of Home.tsx file before component definition

constraints: Do not modify immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
Use single-actor architecture with all Motoko logic in backend/main.mo
Comments must be stored as nested structure within Post records, not in separate map
nonGoals: Optimistic UI updates for comments (user requested explicit refresh via fetchPosts)
Real-time updates using WebSockets (polling every 5s is sufficient)
